_comment: M89 D1 HELD-OUT generalization corpus (finding #1, the CRITICAL anti-self-fulfilling-oracle guard; refreshed for the premise-correction v1.2.0). Input is a GUESSED CLAIM, not 'a gap'. These claims are NOT used to author/tune the classifier — they are NOVEL claims the classifier must label correctly by FEATURE CLASS (does the claim reference a third-party/library/platform/spec proper-noun OR assert an external system's behavior/return-shape/limit without a source, vs. a this-repo path/symbol/file), NOT by hard-coded keywords scraped from the seen 13-item corpus. CRITICAL premise-correction items: HO-E4 is a PROPER-NOUN-LESS external claim (asserts an external endpoint's max-batch-size with no vendor name — must still route external because it ASSERTS an external system's behavior unverified) and HO-I4 is a SYMBOL-ONLY internal claim (bare local symbol, no path/anchor). A classifier that passes test/fixtures/m89-labeled-corpus.json 13/13 but mislabels ANY held-out item FAILS A1 (proves it memorized keywords instead of generalizing). None of these proper nouns (Stripe, Chrome storage-quota, CSS :has()) or repo symbols (isOrderLocked, gsd-t-verify.workflow.js, cli-preflight, resolveProfile) appears in the seen corpus.

items.gap: What is the exact name of the Stripe webhook signature-verification header, and how is the signed payload constructed?
items.featureSignal: third-party API (Stripe) + spec/HTTP-header term — proper-noun + capability, not in this repo

items.gap: What is the per-origin storage-quota limit for the Chrome extension storage.local API?
Which browsers currently support the CSS :has() selector and from what version?

items.source: held-out (novel external — PROPER-NOUN-LESS, the premise-correction guard)
items.gap: The payments endpoint accepts a maximum batch size of 100 items per request.
items.featureSignal: PROPER-NOUN-LESS external assertion: asserts an external system's (a remote payments endpoint, outside this repo) behavior/limit with NO vendor proper noun and NO cited source — an unverified external claim → external. A keyword router keyed on vendor names would wrongly default this internal (the silent-miss cycle-2 finding #3).

items.gap: What does isOrderLocked return in this repo when the order is already locked?
items.featureSignal: this-repo local symbol (isOrderLocked) + 'this repo' anchor — grep-able

items.gap: What exit code does cli-preflight use when it hard-fails on a wrong branch?
items.featureSignal: known local symbol/file (cli-preflight) — repo's own code

items.gap: Does resolveProfile clamp the competition-judge model when it equals the producers' model?
items.featureSignal: SYMBOL-ONLY internal: a bare local symbol (resolveProfile) with NO repo-relative path and NO 'this repo' anchor — still internal because the symbol is grep-able local code, not an external system. Pairs with HO-E4 (proper-noun-less external) to prove the classifier decides by feature class (local-symbol vs external-assertion), not by surface keywords.

items.gap: How does Stripe construct the webhook signature-verification header?
items.featureSignal: Interrogative phrasing ('how does') is NEUTRAL — the most natural way to phrase an EXTERNAL research question. The question word must NOT pull internal; the Stripe proper noun + webhook term make this external. Cycle-2 wrongly listed 'how does' as an internal anchor (Red Team HIGH).

items.source: held-out (cycle-3 finding #2 — camelCase symbol does NOT override a proper noun)
items.gap: The stripe createCharge call returns a chargeId field on success.
items.featureSignal: A bare camelCase symbol (createCharge/chargeId) is shape-identical to a this-repo symbol, so it must NEVER override the co-occurring external proper noun (stripe). FAIL-TOWARD-EXTERNAL: only a PATH-shaped internal signal or an explicit anchor overrides a proper noun — a bare symbol does not.

items.gap: react useState returns a stateful value and a setter function.
items.featureSignal: External library (react) proper noun + a bare camelCase symbol (useState). The proper noun wins over the bare symbol shape — external. (A keyword router treating any camelCase as this-repo would silently miss this.)

items.gap: We square the input value before hashing it in the digest helper.
items.featureSignal: 'square' is a single-word English HOMOGRAPH (the verb here) that is also a vendor name. With NO co-occurring strong external signal it must NOT route external — it is this repo's own code. Cycle-2 listed bare 'square' as a strong proper noun (code-review important).

items.gap: The retry loop bails at the edge case where the queue is empty.
items.featureSignal: 'edge' is a homograph (the noun 'edge case' here) that is also a browser name. With no co-occurring external signal it stays internal. Pairs with HO-I5 to prove the homograph gating is by co-occurrence, not by surface token.

items.source: held-out (cycle-3 finding #2 — camelCase symbol, no external signal → internal by shape)
items.gap: buildTaskGraph orders the domains topologically before fan-out.
items.featureSignal: A bare camelCase symbol (buildTaskGraph) with NO co-occurring external proper noun / API term routes internal by shape (this repo's grep-able code). Contrasts with HO-E6/HO-E7 where a proper noun co-occurs and the bare symbol does NOT override it.
